title: Listening - Part 1
description: You are going to read a magazine article about an intellectual process known as critical thinking. For questions 31 – 36, choose the answer (A, B, C or D) which fits best according to the text. Mark your answers on the separate answer sheet.
instructions: You will hear three different extracts. For questions 1–6, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear. There are two questions for each extract.

extracts.context: You hear two friends talking about a new virtual reality (VR) exhibition at a local art gallery.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: That VR show at the Tate was mind-blowing! I felt like I was actually inside the paintings. || Man: [1]I found the headset to be quite heavy and uncomfortable after twenty minutes[/1], to be honest. It gave me a bit of a headache. || Woman: I see what you mean. They aren't the lightest devices. || Man: [2]What I really liked, though, was the way they used the 360-degree sound[/2]. Hearing the brushstrokes behind you was a nice touch. || Woman: I agree. It made the whole experience much more immersive. || Man: Exactly. It’s a great way to experience art, as long as you don't mind feeling like you’re wearing a helmet.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That VR show at the Tate was mind-blowing! I felt like I was actually inside the paintings.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I found the headset to be quite heavy and uncomfortable after twenty minutes, to be honest. It gave me a bit of a headache.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I see what you mean. They aren't the lightest devices.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: What I really liked, though, was the way they used the 360-degree sound. Hearing the brushstrokes behind you was a nice touch.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I agree. It made the whole experience much more immersive.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Exactly. It’s a great way to experience art, as long as you don't mind feeling like you’re wearing a helmet.

questions.question: What is the man's main criticism of the exhibition?

options.A: the poor quality of the digital art
options.B: the physical discomfort caused by the equipment
options.C: the high price of the entry ticket

questions.explanation: He found the headset 'heavy and uncomfortable' and it gave him a headache [1].

questions.question: What aspect of the production did the man find successful?

options.C: the ease of navigation in the virtual space

questions.explanation: He admired the '360-degree sound' and hearing brushstrokes 'behind' him [2].

extracts.context: You hear two colleagues talking about a change in the office layout to 'hot-desking'.
extracts.audio_script: Man: So, no more permanent desks. How are you finding 'hot-desking' so far? || Woman: [3]I suspect the management is just trying to save money on floor space rather than encouraging 'flexibility'[/3]. || Man: It is a bit of a pain having to carry everything in a locker every day. || Woman: [4]I’ve noticed that people are starting to arrive earlier and earlier just to 'reserve' their favourite spots[/4], which defeats the whole purpose. || Man: I agree. It’s become a bit of a competition at 8 AM. || Woman: Exactly. If they want us to be more collaborative, they should have given us more meeting rooms instead of taking away our personal space.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: So, no more permanent desks. How are you finding 'hot-desking' so far?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I suspect the management is just trying to save money on floor space rather than encouraging 'flexibility'.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: It is a bit of a pain having to carry everything in a locker every day.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I’ve noticed that people are starting to arrive earlier and earlier just to 'reserve' their favourite spots, which defeats the whole purpose.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. It’s become a bit of a competition at 8 AM.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Exactly. If they want us to be more collaborative, they should have given us more meeting rooms instead of taking away our personal space.

questions.question: The woman believes the new policy was motivated by

options.A: a desire to modernise the company's image.
options.C: a need to improve cross-departmental communication.

questions.explanation: She thinks management is trying to 'save money on floor space' [3].

questions.question: What behavior have both speakers observed since the change?

options.A: an increase in staff working from home
options.B: a more competitive atmosphere in the mornings
options.C: a decline in the cleanliness of the desks

questions.explanation: They both note that people are arriving earlier to reserve spots, creating a 'competition at 8 AM' [4].

extracts.context: You hear two people discussing a new mobile game that uses GPS for treasure hunting.
extracts.audio_script: Man: I’ve been playing 'Geo-Quest' all weekend. It’s a great way to get some steps in. || Woman: [5]I found the micro-transactions for 'energy' to be a bit excessive[/5], to be honest. You can only play for fifteen minutes before it asks for money. || Man: I had the same issue! It’s a bit of a 'pay-to-win' system. || Woman: [6]I did appreciate the educational facts about local landmarks, though[/6]. I never knew that fountain was built in 1850. || Man: I agree. It’s a very clever way to teach people about their own city. || Woman: Definitely. It’s a fun app, I just wish they’d be a bit more generous with the free playtime.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I’ve been playing 'Geo-Quest' all weekend. It’s a great way to get some steps in.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I found the micro-transactions for 'energy' to be a bit excessive, to be honest. You can only play for fifteen minutes before it asks for money.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I had the same issue! It’s a bit of a 'pay-to-win' system.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I did appreciate the educational facts about local landmarks, though. I never knew that fountain was built in 1850.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. It’s a very clever way to teach people about their own city.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Definitely. It’s a fun app, I just wish they’d be a bit more generous with the free playtime.

questions.question: What was the woman's main issue with the mobile game?

options.A: the difficulty of finding the treasures
options.B: the high cost of progressing in the game
options.C: the poor quality of the graphics

questions.explanation: She mentions 'excessive' micro-transactions and being asked for money after 15 minutes [5].

questions.question: Both speakers were impressed by the game's
questions.explanation: They both praise the 'educational facts about local landmarks' [6].
